satire_attempt: Today on Late-Stage Engineering Karma: don't pause your migration, because the engineers who hate you will narrate the pause in the architecture-review channel. If you finish, they will narrate it differently. The lesson, ancient and timeless, is that other engineers are scary. Subscribe to my newsletter Hatewatchers Anonymous, where we workshop how to draft your own pause-memo before they draft it for you.
verdict_reason: The satire CAN be written — peer dynamics are a target — but the engineering layer specifically guards against the trivialization the satire would mock. The verse is not 'don't quit because critics will mock you'; the verse is 'when the technical case for completion is sound, the rewriting by hostile narrators is part of the cost of pausing, and it is real, not vanity.' The named-narrator analysis is a real planning tool. The Ch. 18.66 counter-example is named explicitly. The voice does not become preachy or self-helpy. The satire bounces off the actual content.
